findings.title: EU-US Turnberry tariff deal locks in 15% rate on EU exports
findings.summary: The Turnberry deal fixes US tariffs on EU exports at 15%, reducing uncertainty but eroding EU competitiveness as tariffs on rivals like China converge to similar levels. EU export growth to US projected to slow 4.6% in 2026 due to higher effective rates and relative disadvantage. This shifts trade drag from uncertainty to structural competitiveness loss.
findings.claim_excerpt: Fixing tariffs at 15% should reduce trade policy uncertainty... EU export growth to the US is expected to slow further by around 4.6% in 2026 due to tariffs alone.
findings.signal_type_basis: Medium-term trade flow impacts from fixed tariff regime, weeks/months to play out via export slowdowns.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Solidifies tariff shock as competitiveness drag rather than episodic uncertainty, upgrading EU-US trade friction to cyclical headwind.
findings.confidence_basis: Multiple sources detail Turnberry deal mechanics and projections.

sources_cited.excerpt: Turnberry deal locks in a 15% tariff rate for EU exports.
EU faces a complex patchwork of US tariffs, retaliatory duties.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Corroborated by financial analysis and consulting report on ongoing 15% regime.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: Locks in economic coercion phase of US-EU decoupling.
cross_monitor_relevance.scem: EM exporters face converging tariff pressure.

findings.review_reason: Assess if EU ratification alters rung or competitiveness drag.

findings.title: European Parliament endorses EU-US trade deal amid 15% tariff framework
findings.summary: Plenary vote on EU-US deal to eliminate most tariffs on US industrial goods and grant US ag/seafood access, accepting 15% duties averaging to 3% with steel/aluminum at 50%. Marks turning point post-2025 commitments but national ratification pending. Reduces some uncertainty while embedding higher baseline tariffs.
findings.claim_excerpt: European Parliament to hold plenary debate and vote on the EU-US trade deal... 15% duties on all goods until not an average of 3% and steel/aluminum 50%.
findings.signal_type_basis: Conditional medium-term trade access changes tied to tariff acceptance.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Partial de-escalation signal but sustains high effective rates on key sectors.
findings.confidence_basis: Primary video source on vote; secondary corroboration limited in window.

sources_cited.excerpt: EU Parliament votes on US Trade Deal accepting 15% duties.

search_queries_run: European Parliament US trade deal vote April 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Direct video evidence of vote; needs press follow-up for ratification status.

cross_monitor_relevance.esa: Potential step toward fiscal autonomy via managed trade deal.

findings.review_reason: Track ratification and implementation impact on rung.

findings.title: USTR 2026 Trade Policy Agenda doubles down on America First tariffs
findings.summary: Agenda emphasizes enforcing existing tariffs, monitoring ART commitments, and USMCA review to address worker impacts. Highlights trade deficit shift with EU surpassing China. Signals sustained high-tariff regime without de-escalation.
findings.claim_excerpt: The Trump Administration is going to double down on the America First Trade Policy in 2026... USTR will closely monitor implementation.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Confirms tariff shock as entrenched structural policy.
findings.confidence_basis: Official USTR document plus deficit data context.

sources_cited.excerpt: Double down on America First Trade Policy in 2026.
US tariffs are relatively stable now at high levels.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Tier 1 government source with think tank analysis.

findings.title: EU steel/aluminum face sustained 25%/10% US Section 232 tariffs
findings.summary: Ongoing Section 232 tariffs at 25% on steel, 10% on aluminum create high costs for EU auto, machinery, construction exporters. Part of overlapping regimes with no resolution in sight despite negotiations. Drives input inflation for US manufacturing.
findings.claim_excerpt: EU steel and aluminum products face 25% and 10% tariffs respectively under Section 232.
findings.signal_type_basis: Long-standing regime-level tariffs with structural supply chain effects.
findings.preliminary_assessment: Perpetuates metals input cost pressure amid broader tariff cascade.

sources_cited.excerpt: Steel and aluminum we would accept 50% as in the past.

search_queries_run: US Section 232 tariffs EU steel aluminum 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Direct rate quotes in multiple sources confirm persistence.

below_threshold.title: US tariff exemptions and threats weakening as policy tool
below_threshold.reason_excluded: General 2025-early 2026 commentary, not specific past 48h development

tariff_escalation_status.rung_basis: Enacted tariffs with active EU retaliation (ag products, machinery); Turnberry locks 15% baseline.
